brief_summary: Cross-section of a single stabilizer housing on a PCB baseline with three amber numbered callouts. Callout 1 (wire slop) points to the wire-inside-barrel interface. Callout 2 (housing clearance) points to the gap between the stem and bore. Callout 3 (dry hook) points to the wire-hook-in-stem-slot joint. Each callout names the source and the fix.
data_sources: stabilizers-explained.mdx § 'The rattle problem — three sources' — 'Wire slop is the loudest and most immediately obvious. The wire ends rotate inside the housing barrels on every keystroke'
stabilizers-explained.mdx § 'The rattle problem — three sources' — 'Housing clearance refers to the gap between the stabilizer stem and the inside of the housing bore'

stabilizers-explained.mdx § 'The rattle problem — three sources' — 'Lube pooling and dry hook... The wire hooks into a slot at the bottom of each stabilizer stem, and that hook-in-slot joint flexes slightly on every keystroke'
